title: App: Find My Hacker - Live crowd map
description:
## Overview

Real-time map showing GPS positions of all 500 nodes. The hero visual for the demo.

## Features

### Big screen view
- Venue floor plan as background
- 500 dots, updated every few seconds
- Color by: gateway affiliation, battery level, or custom group
- Heatmap overlay toggle

### Search
- Type node name → dot highlights and pulses
- "Find my friend" use case
- QR code on each node links to its position

### Privacy
- Opt-out button on node (hold button 3 sec)
- Opted-out nodes disappear from map
- No historical tracking (current position only)

## Protocol

### Node → Gateway (every 30 sec)
CoAP POST /position
```json
{"lat": 36.1699, "lon": -115.1398, "alt": 0, "hdop": 1.2}
```

### Gateway → Dashboard (WebSocket)
```json
{"node": "abc123", "lat": 36.1699, "lon": -115.1398, "ts": 1234567890}
```

## Tech stack

- Frontend: Leaflet.js or Mapbox GL
- Backend: Python, aggregate from gateways
- Node: GPS read + CoAP POST, already in firmware

## Performance

- 500 nodes × 1 update/30s = 17 updates/sec
- Dashboard batches, renders at 10 FPS
- Stale dots fade after 60 sec no-update
close_reason: Added POST handler to /sensors/location for SenML crowd map positions (live map demo, HAL submit stub, matches deaddrop/coap patterns). OSCORE via service.
